org.name: Northwind
org.description: Issue tracking and project planning for software teams

selected.title: Northwind Agent is now generally available
selected.content: Northwind Agent moves out of beta. It reads an issue, proposes a plan, and opens a draft pull request linked back to the issue. Teams can scope which repositories the agent may touch and require human approval before any branch is pushed. Agent runs now show a step-by-step trace so reviewers can see what it read and why it acted. Available on all paid plans today.

selected.title: Saved triage views
selected.content: You can now save a filtered triage view and share it with your team. Views combine assignee, label, priority, and a time window, and they update live as issues change. A shared view shows the same ordering for everyone, so standups stop drifting. Views are pinned to the left sidebar and can be set as a team default.

selected.title: Webhook delivery retries and a dead-letter log
selected.content: Outbound webhooks now retry with exponential backoff for up to 24 hours, and any delivery that exhausts its retries lands in a per-workspace dead-letter log you can inspect and replay. Each attempt records the response status and latency. This replaces the previous fire-and-forget delivery that silently dropped failures.

selected.title: northwind-sdk v2.4.0
selected.content: Adds typed helpers for the saved-views API and a small client for replaying dead-letter webhook events. No breaking changes. Drops Node 18 from the support matrix; Node 20+ is required.

selected.title: northwind-sdk v2.4.1
selected.content: Patch release. Fixes a pagination cursor bug in listIssues() that could skip the last page when the page size evenly divided the result count.
